problem_addressed: Surgical phase recognition requires expensive full annotations where surgeons mark precise start/end times for each phase, creating a barrier for practical deployment.
core_claim: Timestamp supervision (single timestamp per phase) with uncertainty-aware temporal diffusion achieves competitive performance to full supervision while reducing annotation cost by 74%.
key_contributions: Introduces timestamp supervision paradigm for surgical phase recognition
Proposes Uncertainty-Aware Temporal Diffusion (UATD) method to generate pseudo labels from timestamps
Demonstrates loop training strategy to handle long videos and class imbalance
novelty: UATD uniquely combines temporal diffusion guided by uncertainty estimation to expand single timestamps to high-confidence neighboring frames, avoiding boundary ambiguity while leveraging the consecutive nature of surgical phases.
method_summary: Uses Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, diffuses timestamp labels to adjacent low-uncertainty frames, and employs loop training to optimize spatial and temporal extractors separately using only labeled frames.
method_components: Uncertainty estimation via Monte Carlo Dropout
Temporal diffusion algorithm with uncertainty thresholding
key_innovation: Temporal diffusion stops at high-uncertainty frames (boundaries), ensuring generated pseudo labels are high-confidence and phase-consistent, enabling effective learning from minimal supervision.
assumptions: Surgical phases are long events with consecutive frames
Uncertainty estimation via Monte Carlo Dropout reliably identifies high-confidence frames
Surgeons tend to annotate timestamps near phase midpoints
theoretical_grounding: No formal proofs provided; empirical validation through extensive experiments showing competitive results to full supervision methods.
stated_limitations: The method is specifically designed for surgical videos where phases are long events, which might limit its applicability to other medical image classification tasks.
unstated_weaknesses: The method's performance under significant label noise is not addressed.
The method's reliance on temporal information might not be suitable for static medical images.
The paper doesn't discuss how the method handles variations in surgical procedures or surgeons.
threats_to_validity: The method's performance might be specific to surgical videos where phases are long events, which might not generalize to other medical imaging scenarios.
The effectiveness of the method under substantial label noise is not addressed.
The paper doesn't discuss how the method handles variations in surgical procedures or surgeons.
open_questions: How does the UATD method perform under significant label noise?
Can the method be adapted for static medical images?
How does the method handle variations in surgical procedures or surgeons?
future_work: Adapting the method for static medical images.
Evaluating the method's performance under significant label noise.
Handling variations in surgical procedures or surgeons.
gaps_this_enables: Developing uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection methods for semi-supervised learning in medical image classification.
Exploring timestamp supervision as a cost-effective alternative to full annotations.
Adapting temporal-based methods to static medical images.
relevance_to_topic: The paper introduces an uncertainty-aware method (UATD) for generating pseudo labels, which aligns with the research project on uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection for semi-supervised medical image classification under label noise.
limitations_stated: The method is specifically designed for surgical videos where phases are long events, which might limit its applicability to other medical image classification tasks.
title: Less Is More: Surgical Phase Recognition From Timestamp Supervision
